title: WHATWG-Informed HTML Trees, Cheerio, parse5, and Parser Differentials · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds Cheerio tree-construction evidence adapter from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of Cheerio tree-construction evidence adapter while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Distinguish source tokens, parse5-repaired tree, Cheerio selections, browser DOM, accessibility tree, and rendered state.
Choose document, fragment, HTML, or XML loading options deliberately and test wrapper insertion and case behavior.
Test misnesting, omitted elements, tables, templates, foreign content, duplicate attributes, and foster parenting.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Source Tree Distinct…
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 9275 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove Cheerio loading modes, malformed tree repair, parser differentials, budgets, and source-tree distinctions; every Promise, stream, listener, timer, socket, file, browser context, and report sink needs one cleanup owner; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Source Tree Distinct….
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish source tokens, parse5-repaired tree, cheerio selections, browser dom, accessibility tree, and rendered state. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Source Tree Distinct…, keep Source Tree Distinction connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.
